steps.title: Follow the contrast
steps.content: The word but shows that Patel is pushing back against abandoning polling after prediction failures. The next sentence explains why: polling still helps campaigns decide where their efforts can be effective.

steps.title: Reject illogical actions
steps.content: Choice A means make misleading, which is not the issue. Choice B means establish, but polling already exists. Choice D means add to, the opposite of dropping polling entirely. The needed word means disregard or fail to use. The answer is C.
